California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
May 4,
1994
John Howard
Senior Corporate Counsel
Tenneco IflCb
701 Penny1vania Avenue,

Washington,
D.C.
20004
Re:
Your Request for
Confirmation of Telephone
Advice
Our File No.
1—94—143
Dear Mr.
Howard:
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act.
1
Since your request is one for general
information, we are treating your request as one for informal
assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).
2
Your letter dated April 20,
1994, correctly summarizes my
advice.
Based on the information you have provided,
Tenneco
Employees Good Government Fund
(TEGGF) will have no filing
obligation this year under the Act if it makes contributions of
less than $10,000 to California state and local candidates or
committees.
Federal political, action committees with no prior
histàry of making political cortributions to state or local
candidates or committees have no filing obligations it their total
contributions are less that $10,000 in the first calendar year in
1
Government Code Sections 81060—91015.
. All statutory
references are to the GOvernment Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
g.
All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114;
2 Cal.
Code of Regs.
Sections
18329(c) (3).)
428 J Street, Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


Howard,
John
1—94—143

which they make contributions in California.
However,
once a
federal political action committee establishes a history of making
contributions or expenditures in California, it will qualify as a
California committee in any subsequent year in which it makes
contributions totalling $1,000 or more.
There is no threshold for
establishing a history of political activity.
We are enclosing, as you requested,
a set of the curent
forms and manuals to enable TEGGF to comply with any future
reporting obligations.
Also enclosed is a fact sheet which
suiiuuarizes the qualification criteria for federal political action
committees.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at

322—5662.

enclosures

sections.conclusion: Based on the information you have provided, Tenneco Employees Good Government Fund (TEGGF) will have no filing obligation this year under the Act if it makes contributions of less than $10,000 to California state and local candidates or committees. Federal political action committees with no prior history of making political contributions to state or local candidates or committees have no filing obligations if their total contributions are less than $10,000 in the first calendar year in which they make contributions in California. However, once a federal political action committee establishes a history of making contributions or expenditures in California, it will qualify as a California committee in any subsequent year in which it makes contributions totalling $1,000 or more. There is no threshold for establishing a history of political activity.
sections.question_synthetic: What are the campaign disclosure filing obligations for Tenneco Employees Good Government Fund (TEGGF), a federal political action committee, under California's Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: TEGGF has no filing obligation if contributions are under $10,000 in the first year. Once a federal PAC establishes a history of California contributions, it becomes a California committee with a $1,000 threshold in subsequent years, with no minimum activity level required to establish such history.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a confirmation of informal telephone advice letter. No formal QUESTION section header, but the advice is clearly stated in the body. Document includes enclosures (forms, manuals, and fact sheet). Marked as informal assistance per Regulation 18329(c), meaning it does not provide immunity to the requestor.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the campaign disclosure filing obligations for Tenneco Employees Good Government Fund (TEGGF), a federal political action committee, under California's Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: Based on the information you have provided, Tenneco Employees Good Government Fund (TEGGF) will have no filing obligation this year under the Act if it makes contributions of less than $10,000 to California state and local candidates or committees. Federal political action committees with no prior history of making political contributions to state or local candidates or committees have no filing obligations if their total contributions are less than $10,000 in the first calendar year in which they make contributions in California. However, once a federal political action committee establishes a history of making contributions or expenditures in California, it will qualify as a California committee in any subsequent year in which it makes contributions totalling $1,000 or more. There is no threshold for establishing a history of political activity.

However, once a federal political action committee establishes a history of making contributions or expenditures in California, it will qualify as a California committee in any subsequent year in which it makes contributions totalling $1,000 or more. There is no threshold for establishing a history of political activity. We are enclosing, as you requested, a set of the curent forms and manuals to enable TEGGF to comply with any future reporting obligations. Also enclosed is a fact sheet which suiiuuarizes the qualification criteria for federal political action committees. - If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322—5662. Sincerely, Wayne P. Imberi • Political Reform Consultant enclosures
embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms telephone advice to Tenneco regarding campaign disclosure obligations for its federal PAC. TEGGF has no filing requirement if first-year contributions are under $10,000, but becomes subject to California reporting rules once it establishes any history of California political activity.
